EntityResult.TITLE: Microsoft Exchange Server Multiple Vulnerabilities (ProxyShell) (unauthenticated)
EntityResult.DIAGNOSIS:
Microsoft Exchange Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities including remote code and privilege escalation.

Microsoft released the following details and the security updates.
Affected Versions:
Microsoft Exchange Server 2013 Cumulative Update 23
Microsoft Exchange Server 2019 Cumulative Update 9
Microsoft Exchange Server 2019 Cumulative Update 10
Microsoft Exchange Server 2016 Cumulative Update 20
Microsoft Exchange Server 2016 Cumulative Update 21

QID Detection Logic (unauthenticated):
The QID checks for vulnerable versions of exchange which is retrieved via a GET request to URI "autodiscover/autodiscover.json?@test.com/ecp/?&Email=autodiscover/autodiscover.json%3F@test.com".
The QID also checks for vulnerable instances via GET request "autodiscover/autodiscover.json?@test.com/mapi/nspi/?&Email=autodiscover/autodiscover.json%3F@test.com". Vulnerable instance will return "NT AUTHORIT SYSTEM" in the response.
EntityResult.CONSEQUENCE: Successful exploitation allows attackers to execute remote code.
EntityResult.SOLUTION:
Customers are advised to refer to KB5004780, KB5004779, KB5004778 for information pertaining to this vulnerability.

Patch:
Following are links for downloading patches to fix the vulnerabilities:

KB5004778

KB5004779
